questionHtml: Two of the brightest objects in the night sky seen from Earth are the planet Venus and the star Sirius. Explain why the equation b∝AT4d2 is applicable to Sirius but not to Venus.
markschemeHtml:
equation applies to Sirius/stars that are luminous/emit light «from fusion» ✓

but Venus reflects the Sun’s light/does not emit light «from fusion» ✓

 

OWTTE

meta.title: Two of the brightest objects in the night sky seen from Earth are the planet Venus and the star Sirius. Explain why the equation b∝AT4d2 is applicable to Sirius but not to Venus.
